title: Mobile App PRD v2
content:
# Mobile App PRD — v2
Owner: Nitesh R | Last updated: Jan 24, 2024 | Status: Deferred to Q4

## Change log
v1 (Nov 2023): Initial scope — iOS and Android MVP, push notifications, dashboard view.
v2 (Jan 24, 2024): Section 4.2 updated — Mobile moved to Future Scope. Decision made Jan 23 in #product-roadmap. Engineering bandwidth redirected to API v2 following $485k ARR renewal risk across Apex, Nordvik, TeleCo.

## 1. Problem statement
PMs and engineers at Finlo customers increasingly work from mobile. A mobile app would improve daily active usage and reduce churn for remote-first teams.

## 2. Goals
Target DAU: 2,000 within 90 days of launch.
Day-7 retention target: 45%.
Reduce churn by 8% for mobile-first customer segments.

## 3. Beta results (updated Jan 2024)
Beta cohort DAU came in at 1,180 — 40% below the 2,000 target. Day-7 retention was 28%, below the 45% target. This data weakened the case for Q2 prioritisation.

## 4. Scope
### 4.1 In scope (original v1)
Push notifications for @mentions and ticket updates.
Dashboard with key metrics.
Ticket status view and comment threads.

### 4.2 Mobile — Future Scope (updated v2)
Mobile app development is deferred to Q4 2024. Primary reason: engineering bandwidth reallocated to API v2 following three enterprise renewal risks totalling $485k ARR (Apex, Nordvik, TeleCo). Secondary reason: beta DAU 40% below projection reduces urgency. This section will be revisited in Q3 planning with updated DAU data.

## 5. Open questions for Q4 revisit
Native iOS/Android vs React Native?
Target power users (PMs) or all roles?
Revised DAU target given beta data?
author: Nitesh R

title: API v2 Platform Specification
content:
# API v2 Platform Specification
Owner: Varun S | Last updated: Feb 21, 2024 | Status: In progress — GA April 14

## Context
API v2 is the primary blocker for three enterprise renewals: Apex Corp ($180k ARR), Nordvik ($95k ARR), TeleCo ($210k ARR). Combined at-risk ARR: $485k. Decision to prioritise API v2 over mobile app made Jan 23, 2024 in #product-roadmap.

## Requirements

### Per-tenant rate limiting
Configurable limits per API key. Default: 1,000 req/min. Enterprise: custom limits up to 50,000 req/min.

### Per-endpoint rate limiting (added Feb 20)
Apex requested per-endpoint configuration so that the search endpoint can have different limits from write endpoints. Rate limit config stored in Redis and checked at the API gateway layer. Added 2 weeks to the timeline — revised GA: April 14, 2024. Original target was March 31.

### Webhook delivery
At-least-once delivery guarantee. Exponential backoff retry: 5 retries over 24 hours. HMAC-SHA256 secret signing. Delivery logs retained 72 hours.

### OAuth 2.0 scopes
Scopes: read:tickets, write:tickets, read:users, admin. Per-key scope configuration available in the dashboard.

## Timeline
Rate limiting complete: Feb 14.
Webhook delivery complete: Feb 10.
OAuth scopes complete: Feb 28.
Per-endpoint rate config complete: March 14.
QA and staging: March 15 – April 7.
GA: April 14, 2024.
author: Varun S

title: Pricing Strategy PRD v2
content:
# Pricing Strategy PRD
Owner: Priya M | Version: v2 | Last updated: Feb 2, 2024

## Change log
v1 (Jan 15): Initial analysis — flat per-seat vs usage-based comparison.
v2 (Feb 2): Volume tiers added. Enterprise custom contracts section added. Grandfather period extended from 3 months to 6 months based on customer feedback. Customer comms talking points added.

## Decision
Switching from per-seat flat fee ($49/seat/month) to usage-based pricing ($0.08/API call). Decision made Feb 1, 2024 in #pricing-strategy by Nitesh R. Rationale: 34% projected ARR uplift over 18 months, removes per-seat friction for enterprise procurement, aligns with how enterprise teams buy SaaS.

## Pricing tiers
Starter: 0 to 10k calls/month at $0.08 per call.
Growth: 10k to 100k calls/month at $0.06 per call.
Scale: 100k to 1M calls/month at $0.04 per call.
Enterprise: over 1M calls/month on a custom contract.

## Existing customer migration
6-month grandfather period for all existing customers — current flat-fee pricing maintained until September 2024. 3 customers identified whose usage would cost more under new model — offering custom enterprise contracts. Migration comms to be sent March 1, 2024.

## Customer communication talking points
1. You only pay for what you use — teams that use Finlo heavily get better value as they scale.
2. Your current pricing is locked for 6 months — no changes until September 2024.
3. If your usage means you would pay more, we will reach out personally with a custom plan.

## Why we switched
Three enterprise prospects declined in Q4 2023 due to pricing inflexibility. Glean's $50k+ annual pricing validates enterprise demand but prices out mid-market. Usage-based removes the per-seat objection and fits enterprise procurement patterns.
author: Priya M

title: Q2 2024 Roadmap
content:
# Q2 2024 Roadmap
Owner: Nitesh R | Locked: Feb 5, 2024

## Q2 pillars

### 1. API v2 GA (P0)
Target date: April 14, 2024. Unblocks $485k in enterprise renewals (Apex, Nordvik, TeleCo). Engineering lead: Varun S.

### 2. Usage-based pricing launch (P0)
Target date: May 1, 2024. Switches billing from $49/seat to $0.08/API call with volume tiers. Business lead: Priya M.

### 3. Enterprise SSO (shipped Q1)
SAML 2.0 and Okta integration shipped Jan 30. Nordvik approved. TeleCo review pending.

## Deferred items
Mobile app: deferred to Q4. Beta DAU 40% below target. Engineering bandwidth needed for API v2. Decision made Jan 23 in #product-roadmap.

Dashboard redesign: deferred to Q4. 67% of users requested dark mode (Nov 2023 research) but engineering capacity is fully committed to API v2 and pricing work. Riya continuing design explorations in Q2 so build can start in Q4.

Slack integration v2: Q4 candidate pending Q2 bandwidth review.

## Planning notes
Q2 planning kicked off Jan 4 in #product-roadmap. Mobile app deprioritisation decided Jan 23 after enterprise renewal risk surfaced. Roadmap locked Feb 5 after leadership review.
author: Nitesh R

title: Engineering Architecture Overview
content:
# Finlo Engineering Architecture
Owner: Varun S | Last updated: Feb 2024

## Stack
Backend: Python (FastAPI), PostgreSQL, Redis, Celery.
Frontend: React, TypeScript.
Infrastructure: AWS (ECS, RDS, ElastiCache, MSK).
Search: Elasticsearch for full-text. ChromaDB for semantic search (new in API v2).

## Key architectural decisions

### PostgreSQL over MongoDB (Dec 2023)
We evaluated MongoDB for ticket storage but chose PostgreSQL. Reasons: (1) our data is highly relational — tickets, comments, users, and orgs all have FK relationships; (2) the team has deeper PostgreSQL expertise; (3) MongoDB's flexible schema would make the billing metering pipeline harder to query precisely. Decision made by Varun S and Arjun K after a 2-week spike.

### Kafka for usage metering (Feb 2024)
Real-time usage metering pipeline uses Kafka (AWS MSK) for event streaming and ClickHouse for aggregation. Required for usage-based pricing. Handles 12k events/second at p99 latency of 38ms.

### Celery and Redis for async jobs (Q3 2023)
Background job processing — email, exports, webhook delivery — uses Celery with Redis as the broker. Chosen over SQS for local dev parity and simpler retry config.
author: Varun S

title: Competitor Analysis — Q1 2024
content:
# Competitor Analysis
Owner: Nitesh R | Last updated: Jan 16, 2024

## Summary
No tool at an accessible price point ($10–15/user/month) traces decisions across PRDs, Jira, and Slack. Glean comes closest on cross-tool retrieval but is enterprise-only at $50k+ annually and has no decision lineage feature.

## Glean
Strengths: best-in-class semantic retrieval, 100+ connectors, fast.
Weakness: enterprise-only pricing ($50k+ annual minimum), no decision tracing.
Recent: $100M Series D in Jan 2024 at $1.2B valuation — doubling down on Fortune 500.
Our opportunity: Glean leaves the 20–500 employee mid-market entirely unserved.

## Confluence AI
Strengths: native Atlassian integration, no setup friction.
Weakness: siloed to Confluence only — misses Slack and standalone PRDs entirely.

## Notion AI
Strengths: clean UX, excellent for Notion-native teams.
Weakness: useless if team uses Jira. No cross-tool synthesis.

## Finlo positioning
Our wedge is decision tracing across tools at mid-market pricing. Not just cross-tool search — the why behind the what. Surfacing the Slack thread that preceded the PRD change that closed the Jira ticket. Price target: $10–15/user/month. Glean for PMs at 1/10th the price.
author: Nitesh R

title: Enterprise Customer Playbook
content:
# Enterprise Customer Playbook
Owner: Sanya R | Last updated: Feb 2024

## Active enterprise accounts

### Apex Corp — $180k ARR
Renewal due: April 2024.
Key requirement: API v2 with per-endpoint rate limiting.
Secondary ask: CSV/JSON data export (shipped in FINLO-145).
Champion: Marcus D, Head of Engineering.

### Nordvik — $95k ARR
Renewal due: March 2024.
Key requirement: SAML 2.0 SSO (shipped in FINLO-67).
Also requires API v2 before full renewal commitment.
Champion: Lena H, VP Product.

### TeleCo — $210k ARR
Renewal due: April 2024.
Key requirements: API v2 and SAML SSO.
SAML review with TeleCo IT scheduled for next week.
Champion: Raj P, CTO.

## Renewal strategy
All three accounts are contingent on API v2 GA on April 14. SSO is shipped. Export is shipped. API v2 is the single remaining blocker.
author: Sanya R

title: Q3 2024 Planning Notes
content:
# Q3 2024 Planning Notes
Owner: Nitesh R | Status: Early draft — planning begins June 2024

## Carry-forward from Q2
Items deferred from Q2 that are candidates for Q3 or Q4:
Mobile app (Q4 candidate — depends on API v2 outcome and revised DAU projections).
Dashboard redesign (Q4 — design explorations completing in Q2).
Slack integration v2 (Q3 candidate — customer demand growing).

## Potential Q3 themes
Expansion and retention: deepen enterprise features post API v2 GA.
Self-serve growth: improve onboarding funnel for SMB customers.
Platform reliability: observability improvements and SLA hardening.

## Open questions
Will API v2 GA on April 14 free up enough bandwidth for mobile in Q4?
What does usage-based pricing adoption look like by June?
Should we invest in a Slack app for Finlo in Q3?
author: Nitesh R
